persistence_invariants.brief: The split Alembic revision applies (add batch_number + serial_number, backfill by delimiter, drop inventory_code) and reverses cleanly on the branch; the down path reconstructs inventory_code from location + batch + serial. Every pre-existing row survives (AC1/AC3/AC4/AC6, R1).
The add-columns + backfill + drop-column sequence runs in one transaction; the branch is never left half-migrated, and canonical location/quantity are never altered (AC3, R1).
The composite UNIQUE(sku, location) inherited from F1 remains intact after the column split; no duplicate (sku, location) pair is introduced by the migration (R3).

nfrs.brief: Every existing stock row survives the split migration with no loss or corruption: conforming codes backfill batch/serial (AC1), nonconforming stay NULL not dropped (AC2), all rows survive with location/quantity unchanged (AC3), and the migration reverses cleanly (AC6). Defended by PI1 and PI2.
nfrs.fitness_function: Real-branch integration test: seed conforming + nonconforming rows on the paired branch, run the split revision, assert backfilled values, NULLs, and full row survival; run the down revision and assert inventory_code is restored.

nfrs.brief: N/A for this feature. R2 (no negative / no overcommit) is a quantity write-path guarantee; the split migration and the detail view do not change quantity semantics, so the existing CHECK quantity >= 0 and service-level checks are preserved unchanged, not re-implemented here.
nfrs.fitness_function: N/A: no quantity-mutating code path in this feature; the inherited F1 quantity fitness test continues to defend R2.

nfrs.brief: The (sku, location) composite uniqueness (PI3) survives the column split; splitting inventory_code out does not touch the addressing key or introduce duplicate rows (R3). location stays canonical and is not derived from the code's leading segment.
nfrs.fitness_function: Real-branch integration test: after the migration, assert UNIQUE(sku, location) still holds and a duplicate-pair insert is still rejected.

nfrs.brief: All integration tests (schema diff, backfill, NULL-handling, nonconforming count, up/down round-trip) run against the paired Lakebase branch (databricks_postgres), never a mock or in-memory substitute; CI refuses to merge a PR whose integration tests do not run against a real branch.
nfrs.fitness_function: pytest-bdd suite binds to the branch DB via conftest fixtures, Alembic migrations applied to the branch first, FK-aware targeted-DELETE cleanup; CI gate asserts the real-branch connection is used.

nfrs.brief: The SKU detail view renders batch and serial as distinct fields within the existing React + TypeScript SPA under client/ with no full-page reload; the boundary returns JSON exposing batch_number and serial_number (boundary renders_via: react), not server-rendered HTML. The client ships its own component tests; the API is covered by the R4 branch tests.
nfrs.fitness_function: Layering/config fitness test: the boundary returns JSON; client/ Vitest + Playwright harness covers the detail view rendering split fields with no full-page reload.

nfrs.brief: PO preference (additive-where-possible): the new batch_number/serial_number columns are added and backfilled before the combined column is dropped, so old reads keep working through the backfill; the drop is the final step. Architect-proposed beyond the Required brief.
nfrs.fitness_function: Real-branch test asserts the two new columns exist and are populated for conforming rows before inventory_code is dropped within the same revision.

nfrs.brief: PO preference: a row whose batch_number or serial_number is NULL renders an explicit "none" / not-tracked state in the detail view, never a null crash or a blank region, and never resurrects the dropped combined code. Architect-proposed beyond the Required brief.
nfrs.fitness_function: client/ component test: a SKU with NULL batch/serial renders the explicit none state; no crash, no blank field.

nfrs.brief: DB connection and Alembic env resolve from the injected DATABASE_URL (paired branch, database databricks_postgres); no hardcoded DSN and no app-specific DB name (twelve-factor config-in-env). Architect-proposed beyond the Required brief.
nfrs.fitness_function: Config-in-env fitness test: no hardcoded DSN; app and Alembic env read the injected DATABASE_URL and connect to databricks_postgres on the paired branch.
